footer.tagline: The financial infrastructure for African travellers heading to Europe. Account, attestations, insurance, through the front door.
footer.disclaimer: Treepi provides financial services and attestations that comply with the documentary requirements of visa applications. Visa decisions belong exclusively to consular authorities; no Treepi service constitutes a promise or guarantee that a visa will be granted. Client funds are deposited with a regulated partner institution in the European Union, in segregated individual accounts. Prices include tax and may change; the current price list is shown before any subscription.
footer.dataBody: The information collected on this site is used solely to keep you informed about the launch of Treepi. You can exercise your rights by writing to
footer.prelaunch: Treepi is currently in its pre-launch phase.
footer.purpose: The sole purpose of this site is to present the concept and gather the interest of future users. The information published has no contractual value. Treepi reserves the right to modify or update the content of this site at any time.
footer.ipNotice: All visual and textual elements on this site are protected by intellectual property rights and may not be reproduced without prior authorisation.

hero.subtitle: Open your euro account from your country. Deposit your funds and get the financial guarantee attestation that shows the consulate your stay is funded. Add your travel insurance, and the visa appeal cover that defends you if you're refused.
trust: Funds held at a regulated institution in Europe, in an account in your name
Attestations verifiable online by the consulate in 30 seconds
Schengen travel insurance at the lowest price, €30 / month, €60 / 3 months
Visa appeal insurance: legal protection to defend you if you're refused

problem.statCaption: Up to 6 out of 10 Schengen visa applications are refused in some African countries: 62.8% in the Comoros, 51.9% in Senegal, versus a 14.8% worldwide average (2025). (Sources: European Commission, LAGO Collective)
problem.body: Up to 6 out of 10 applications are refused in Africa, versus barely 1 in 10 on average in the rest of the world, most often for lack of credible proof of funds. A simple local bank statement isn't enough: it can be considered unreliable or temporarily topped up. Treepi gives you a document the consulate can verify, real funds, in a European account in your name. And if your visa is unfairly refused, the appeal insurance activates your legal protection to defend you.

And if your visa is unfairly refused, the appeal insurance saves you from losing everything: our immigration lawyers defend your application.

recours.title: Visa appeal insurance
recours.body: That's what African travellers lost in 2024 in refused application fees, refusals that are sometimes arbitrary, with no real explanation, and money never refunded. With appeal insurance you no longer just take it: our immigration lawyers challenge the decision and defend your application all the way.

insurances.banner: Visa refused? Your travel insurance is refunded upon presentation of the refusal, and with the appeal insurance, your challenge is covered.

globe.subtitle: Because travelling should never be a privilege, we are committed to giving African travellers credible, accessible and secure solutions so that every desire to travel becomes possible.

faq.title: The questions we actually get asked

items.question: Does Treepi guarantee that I'll get my visa?
items.answer: No, and beware of anyone who promises you that. The decision belongs exclusively to the consulate. What Treepi guarantees is the compliance of your documents: real funds in a European account in your name and an attestation verifiable online, which answer the number 1 reason for refusals.

items.question: Are my funds safe? Can I get them back?
items.answer: Yes. Your funds are deposited with a regulated institution in the European Union, in a segregated account in your name, never part of Treepi's assets. Visa granted or refused, they are returned to you in full, released within 72 h of the consulate's decision.

items.question: Is there a Treepi bank card?
items.answer: Not yet: no payment card is offered at launch. The Treepi card is coming soon, in the meantime, your account is used to receive transfers, prepare your attestation and fund your stay once your visa is granted.

items.question: Why can't I send a transfer before my visa?
items.answer: That's the whole point of the attestation: the funds for your stay remain deposited while your application is being processed, which makes your document credible in the consulate's eyes. Outgoing transfers are enabled as soon as you present your visa.

items.question: How do I top up my account?
items.answer: Three methods: cash deposit at a local partner branch, bank transfer, or card. From XOF, XAF and other African currencies to your European IBAN, rates and fees displayed before every confirmation, credited within 24 h.

items.question: What happens if my visa is refused?
items.answer: Your funds are returned to you in full within 72 h and your travel insurance is refunded upon presentation of the refusal. If you subscribed to the appeal insurance, our immigration lawyers review your notice and challenge the decision, costs covered up to €15,000.

items.question: What exactly is the financial guarantee attestation?
items.answer: A financial document certifying that the funds required for your stay are actually deposited and blocked in a European account in your name. Its amount matches the official scales of your destination country and the consulate can verify it online in 30 seconds.

items.question: How much does the account cost?
items.answer: Opening is free. The account alone costs €100 in annual management fees, and if you subscribe to the financial guarantee attestation (€250), the management fees are waived.

items.question: How quickly do I get my documents?
items.answer: The travel insurance attestation arrives by e-mail within minutes of subscribing. The financial guarantee attestation is issued within 24 to 48 h of receiving your funds, in digital format, ready to add to your file.

finalCta.title: Your journey starts with your account.
finalCta.subtitle: Open it in 5 minutes. Top up whenever you want. Your attestation awaits.

trust.title: A serious account, in your name.
trust.body: Treepi provides its services through an electronic money institution licensed by the ACPR (Banque de France). Your account is protected by recognised security standards, and your funds are segregated, never part of Treepi's assets.

items.question: Is it a real European IBAN?
items.answer: Yes. Once your account is validated, you receive an IBAN in your name, issued by our regulated partner in France, usable for receiving international transfers.

items.question: Do I need to live in Europe to open it?
items.answer: No, that's the whole point of Treepi. You open your account from your country in Africa, 100% online, with just your African passport.

items.question: How long does opening take?
items.answer: 24 h on average: five online steps from your phone, and your European IBAN is ready to use.

items.question: How do I top up my account?
items.answer: By cash deposit at a local partner branch, bank transfer or card, from XOF, XAF and other African currencies to your account, fees displayed before confirmation.

items.question: Is there a bank card with this account?
items.answer: Not yet at launch, the Treepi card is coming soon. Your account is fully functional today for top-ups, incoming transfers and your attestation.

items.question: Why can't I send a transfer before my visa?
items.answer: Because your funds serve as a guarantee while your application is processed: that hold is what makes your attestation credible. Outgoing transfers are enabled as soon as you present your visa.

items.question: What happens to my funds if my visa is refused?
items.answer: They are returned to you in full, released within 72 h of the consulate's decision.

items.question: Which top-up methods are available?
items.answer: Three: bank card (instant), cash deposit at a local partner branch (credited within 24 h) and local or SWIFT bank transfer (72 h to 2 weeks).

items.question: What are the real timeframes?
items.answer: Card: instant. Cash deposit: about 24 h after your receipt is received. SWIFT transfer: from 72 h to 2 weeks depending on intermediary banks, plan ahead if your visa application is coming up.

items.question: Which currencies can I top up in?
items.answer: XOF and XAF at the guaranteed fixed rate (€1 = 655.957 FCFA), in euros, and in other African currencies depending on partner deposit points.

items.question: Are there fees? When are they shown?
items.answer: Each method has its fees, always shown before confirmation, you see the debited amount, the fees and what lands in your account before you confirm. Never fees discovered afterwards.

items.question: Why is a receipt required?
items.answer: For cash deposits, the photo of your deposit proof secures the operation and speeds up validation: it links your deposit to your Treepi reference.

items.question: How do I know where my top-up is?
items.answer: Every top-up has its timeline in your space (initiated → received → credited) and you're notified by e-mail and WhatsApp at every step.

items.question: Can a relative in Europe top up my account?
items.answer: Yes, by card or by transfer to your IBAN, with your reference in the description. It's the simplest way for a sponsor to fund your stay without touching their own file.

finalCta.title: Ready to fund your journey?
finalCta.subtitle: Top up your account in minutes, from your country, every fee shown before confirmation.

meta.title: Financial guarantee attestation · Treepi
meta.description: The proof of funds that truly reassures the consulate for your Schengen visa.

hero.subtitle: A local bank statement leaves room for doubt. Our attestation proves your funds are there, and blocked during your application.

problem.subtitle: One of the main reasons for visa refusals is the consulates' doubt about your real financial capacity.

how.subtitle: Your journey starts here! Pack your bags, we take care of everything to put every chance on your side so your trip happens.

steps.title2: from the comfort of your country in Africa
steps.description: We all need an account in the right currency to travel. Treepi offers a European account for non-residents, accessible anytime and anywhere: an account in your name to deposit money, receive transfers and prepare your stay, without hidden fees.

From XAF, XOF and several other African currencies to your euro account within 24 h. Top up the amount you need to fund your entire stay in Europe, by cash deposit in local currency or by transfer, from Africa to your European IBAN.

steps.title1: Get a reliable document proving to the consulate that you can
steps.description: A simple bank statement is not, in itself, proof of funds: it can be seen as unconvincing or temporarily topped up. With Treepi, no more stress proving your resources: your file rests on a reliable, verifiable financial document that puts every chance on your side.

rows.title: Credibility
rows.you: Far more solid than a statement: it certifies both the presence AND the blocking of the funds.
rows.consulate: The funds are already in Europe and blocked during processing, impossible to withdraw, unlike a simple statement.

rows.title: Compliant
rows.you: The European account is opened in your name: it's your money, not an intermediary's.
rows.consulate: Meets the transparency and traceability requirements expected of a financial document.

rows.title: Fast & secure
rows.you: Issued within 24 h, in digital format, ready to attach to your file.
rows.consulate: Authenticity verifiable in 1 minute, via QR code or dedicated interface.

offer.body: As soon as you subscribe to your financial guarantee attestation, the opening of your European account is included, for free. One subscription, two benefits: a stronger visa file, and an account ready for the rest of the journey.

tabs.label: Tourism
tabs.title: Tourism
tabs.description: Show that you have the means needed for the length of your tourist stay: accommodation and expenses included.

tabs.label: Family visit
tabs.title: Family visit
tabs.description: Reassure the consulate about your ability to fund your stay, even when hosted by a relative, the attestation complements the host certificate.

tabs.label: Business trip
tabs.title: Business trip
tabs.description: Complement your employer's documents with solid, verifiable personal proof of funds.

tabs.label: Medical
tabs.title: Medical reasons
tabs.description: Prove the funding of your stay and related costs for a medical trip, with funds actually blocked.

duo.subtitle: The attestation doesn't replace your bank statement: the consulate will always ask for it. It comes as a complement, and it's this duo that gives your Schengen visa application its best chances.

bank.title: Your bank statement
bank.description: It shows your history and the regularity of your income over recent months. Whatever happens, you'll need to provide it with your application.

treepi.flag: The complement that makes the difference
treepi.title: The Treepi attestation
treepi.description: It certifies what your statement can't prove: the funds for your stay are actually deposited and blocked, in a European account in your name.

duo.note: = a complete financial file. The history of your statement + the certainty of the attestation: it's this combination that gives your Schengen visa application its best chances.

items.question: Is the attestation recognised by consulates?
items.answer: It complies with the requirements of many European consulates. We regularly update our formats to guarantee their validity. It maximises your chances, without guaranteeing the outcome of the consular decision.

items.question: How is it different from a bank statement?
items.answer: A statement shows a balance at a point in time, which may have been temporarily topped up. The attestation certifies that the funds are actually deposited AND blocked in a European account in your name during processing, and the consulate can verify it online.

items.question: How much do I need to block?
items.answer: The amount depends on your destination country, the length of your stay and your accommodation, consulates apply a daily scale. Use our simulator: it calculates your target from the official scales published by each State.

items.question: What happens to my funds after the decision?
items.answer: Visa granted or refused, your funds are returned to you in full, released within 72 h of the decision. With your visa in hand, they fund your stay through the enabled outgoing transfers.

finalCta.title: Present a flawless file.
finalCta.subtitle: Get an attestation the consulate cannot question.

FCFA conversion at the fixed rate of €1 = 655.957 FCFA (XOF/XAF), for information. Attestation sent by e-mail as soon as payment is confirmed. Refunded upon presentation of a visa refusal.

cards.title: Licensed insurance partner
cards.description: Your attestation is issued with a licensed partner insurer, compliant with European consulate requirements.

cards.title: Secure payment

items.question: Is travel insurance mandatory for a Schengen visa?
items.answer: Yes, in the vast majority of cases. The Visa Code requires minimum medical coverage of €30,000, including repatriation, valid across the whole Schengen area and for the entire stay.

items.question: How is my price calculated?
items.answer: Two plans depending on length: €30 per person up to 30 days, €60 from 31 to 90 days, adjusted by age bracket. No other variable, no hidden fees.

items.question: Does the price increase with age?
items.answer: Slightly, like any travel insurance: +15% from 41 to 60, +35% from 61 to 70, +65% from 71 to 80. Over 81, pricing is custom. Under-18s get -15%.

items.question: What happens if my visa is refused?
items.answer: Your insurance is refunded in full, upon simple presentation of the consulate's refusal notice.

items.question: When do I receive my attestation?
items.answer: Immediately: it's sent by e-mail as soon as payment is confirmed, ready to attach to your visa file.

items.question: Can I subscribe for several travellers at once?
items.answer: Yes, enter the number of travellers in the quote: each person receives their own named attestation, in a single payment.

items.question: My stay exceeds 90 days, what should I do?
items.answer: The plans cover stays up to 90 days (short-stay Schengen visa). Beyond that, it's a national long-stay visa: contact us on WhatsApp for a suitable solution.

finalCta.title: Your insurance, ready in 3 minutes.
finalCta.subtitle: The lowest price on the market, the attestation in your inbox.

meta.title: Visa appeal insurance · Treepi
meta.description: A visa refusal isn't the end: specialised lawyer, costs covered up to €15,000.

hero.title1: A refusal isn't the end. It's the beginning of
hero.subtitle: Even with a good file, a visa is never guaranteed. If you're refused, a specialised lawyer takes over, costs covered up to €15,000.

why.subtitle: Even a solid file can be refused for sometimes questionable reasons. Visa appeal insurance gives you a real second chance, without starting from scratch and without shouldering everything alone.

cards.title: Unjustified refusal
cards.description: Personalised analysis of the reasons and preparation of a reasoned appeal.

cards.title: Procedural flaw
cards.description: Challenging decisions tainted by formal irregularities.

cards.title: Legal support
cards.description: Connection with a lawyer specialised in immigration law.

cards.title: Specialised lawyer
cards.description: who takes over your procedure
Full coverage of appeal costs

cards.title: 2-3 months
cards.description: Average length of the appeal procedure

steps.title: Subscribe before applying
steps.description: You activate your protection upstream, before even filing your visa application.

steps.title: Refusal analysis
steps.description: If refused, our teams study the decision and identify the reasons.

steps.title: Building the appeal file
steps.description: A reasoned file matching the consulate's expectations is prepared.

steps.title: Assistance from a specialised lawyer
steps.description: A lawyer takes charge of your appeal procedure.

steps.title: Follow-up until the decision
steps.description: You are supported until the final appeal decision.

items.question: When should I subscribe?
items.answer: Before filing your visa application. Subscribing upstream means you're protected from the first decision.

items.question: What exactly is covered?
items.answer: The review of your refusal notice and complete file, the preparation of a reasoned appeal, the assistance of a lawyer specialised in immigration law and coverage of procedure costs up to €15,000.

items.question: Is the success of the appeal guaranteed?
items.answer: No, no legal procedure can be, and beware of anyone who promises it. The insurance maximises your chances: your file is defended by a professional, all the way to the final decision.

items.question: How long does the procedure take?
items.answer: On average 2 to 3 months, depending on the jurisdiction and the complexity of the file. You're kept informed at every step.

finalCta.title: Travel with peace of mind.
finalCta.subtitle: Activate your protection before your application, just in case.

items.question: Can I pay before receiving the physical card?
items.answer: Yes. Your virtual card is active from account opening and can be added to Apple Pay or Google Pay to pay contactless immediately.

items.question: Where can I collect my physical card?
items.answer: At your accommodation address in Europe, or at a Treepi pick-up point, available in Paris only for now.

items.question: Are there hidden fees abroad?
items.answer: No. The card is in euros: no automatic conversion, no international surcharge. You see exactly what you spend.

items.question: Is my card secure?
items.answer: Yes: instant freeze/unfreeze from the app, customisable limits, 3D Secure for online payments and real-time spending tracking.

finalCta.title: Your card awaits.
finalCta.subtitle: Order, activate and pay, anywhere in the world.

meta.title: Pricing · Treepi
meta.description: Fair prices, zero surprises: European account, travel insurance and visa appeal insurance.

hero.subtitle: You pay exactly what you see, every fee is announced before you confirm. And if your visa is refused, your funds are returned and your travel insurance is refunded.
hero.note: Payment from Africa: cash deposit, transfer, mobile money, rates and fees shown before confirmation.

compte.title: European account
compte.subtitle: Your European IBAN in your name, opened in 24 h from your country, with just your passport. Opening is free in both cases, only the attestation changes the fees.

solo.badge: For sending and receiving transfers in Europe
solo.title: European account alone
solo.audience: Who's it for? You want to open your account, top it up and receive transfers
features: European IBAN in your name, issued in 24 h
Top-up from Africa, cash deposit or transfer
Receiving international transfers
Outgoing transfers enabled as soon as you show your visa
solo.note: Switch to the attestation whenever you want, management fees become free.

combo.title: Account + Financial guarantee attestation
combo.audience: Who's it for? You need to prove your funds to the consulate, the number 1 reason for visa refusals.
features: The whole European account, management fees waived
Attestation matching the official scales of your destination country
Verifiable online by the consulate in 30 seconds
Issued within 24 to 48 h of receiving your funds
Visa refused? Funds returned in full within 72 h
combo.note: Your funds remain yours, in an account in your name, visa granted or refused.

voyage.title: Schengen travel insurance
voyage.subtitle: The €30,000 coverage required by consulates, emergency care and repatriation, valid across the whole Schengen area. Attestation in your inbox 3 minutes after subscribing. And if your visa is refused, it's refunded.

plan1.title: 1-month plan
plan1.audience: Who's it for? Family visit, tourism or a business trip of a few weeks.
features: €30,000 coverage, medical care & repatriation
Valid in the 29 countries of the Schengen area
Compliant attestation, as a PDF, by e-mail in 3 min
Refunded if your visa is refused

plan3.title: 3-month plan
plan3.audience: Who's it for? Long stay, multiple entries or still-flexible dates, the safety margin consulates appreciate.
features: €30,000 coverage, medical care & repatriation
Valid in the 29 countries of the Schengen area
Compliant attestation, as a PDF, by e-mail in 3 min
Refunded if your visa is refused
Covers the full maximum length of a short-stay visa

compare.title: Same €30,000 coverage, same Schengen compliance, without the traditional insurers' margin.
compare.note: * Orders of magnitude observed on the market, for illustration, the required coverage (€30,000, Schengen area) is identical.

recours.title: Visa appeal insurance
recours.subtitle: Up to 6 out of 10 applications are refused in some African countries, sometimes with no real explanation. With the appeal cover, you no longer just take it.
recours.cardTitle: An unjustified refusal should no longer be the end.
recours.cardBody: A refused visa means lost fees, a project on hold, and almost never a clear justification. Appeal insurance activates your legal protection: our immigration lawyers study your refusal notice and challenge the decision on your behalf.
features: Review of your refusal notice and your complete file
Specialised lawyers in immigration law
Challenge procedure handled end to end
Support until the final decision
recours.priceNote: one-time payment, covers your visa application
recours.note: To subscribe alongside your file, before the consulate's decision.

combos.subtitle: Each product is purchased separately, here are the three most common combinations among Treepers.

cards.badge: I don't want to take any risk
cards.title: Total serenity

trust: Visa refused? Funds returned within 72 h and travel insurance refunded.
Funds deposited at a regulated institution in Europe, in an account in your name.
Attestations verifiable online by the consulate in 30 seconds.
Humans you can reach, WhatsApp 7 days a week, answer within 2 h.

items.question: Are there hidden fees?
items.answer: No. Every fee is shown before you confirm, top-up, attestation, insurance. What you see when paying is exactly what is debited.

items.question: Why are management fees waived with the attestation?
items.answer: Because the attestation is the heart of our service: when you subscribe to it (€250), the €100 annual account management fees are waived, you only pay for your attestation.

items.question: What happens if my visa is refused?
items.answer: Your funds are returned in full within 72 h, your travel insurance is refunded upon presentation of the refusal, and if you subscribed to the appeal insurance, the challenge is covered up to €15,000.

items.question: Does the travel insurance price increase with age?
items.answer: Slightly: -15% under 18, base rate from 18 to 40, then +15% (41-60), +35% (61-70) and +65% (71-80). Beyond that, custom pricing.

items.question: How do I pay from Africa?
items.answer: Cash deposit at a partner branch, bank transfer or mobile money (Orange Money, MTN MoMo), rates and fees shown before every confirmation.

items.question: Is the money deposited in my account a fee?
items.answer: No! The funds you deposit remain yours: they serve as a guarantee for your attestation and then fund your stay. Only the attestation, the insurance and any management fees are costs.

items.question: Can I get the travel insurance without the account?
items.answer: Yes, the Schengen travel insurance can be subscribed independently, in 3 minutes, and the attestation arrives by e-mail. Each product is purchased separately.

finalCta.title: Your budget is clear. Your journey can begin.
finalCta.subtitle: Open your account in 5 minutes, every fee will be shown before you confirm.
